content:
t realize you don’t need to be the shield when you can simply be the ground.

I am the one who stays. My name is Uncle Chen, though the name matters less than the function. For twenty years, I was the man at the stall, the one who exchanged coins for greens and advice for nods. Now, I am the man who fixes the leak in the ceiling and the one who knows exactly which floorboard will groan under the w
